Hey everyone, it's your favorite shy-turned-bitch from bba. So, there I was, studying late in the library at 3rd floor, trying to finish my assignment. This guy, let's call him "Hottie" kept giving me these looks. 
I ignored him at first, but he was persistent. Finally, he walked over and asked if I needed help. 
One thing led to another, and we ended up in a secluded study room. Let's just say, my textbooks got a lot more... interesting that night. Turns out, he holds a good reputed post in the council, and he knows how to use his 🫣 influence both in and out of the classroom.*

I saw her first time in canteen, second year. She was always with her gang, laughing loud, acting like she doesn’t care about anyone. I noticed her way before she noticed me.
Months went like that. Small small things only. She sat few tables away. Once she asked for my charger. Said “thanks yaar” and left. For her it was normal. For me, whole day mood set.
One day we actually talked. Random bakwas like attendance, exams, how college is trash. She said I’m “too seedha” for this college. I laughed, but inside my heart was racing.
That night she replied to my story. Just one word — cute. We talked till 2 AM. No flirting, just vibes.
Then she asked, “Have you ever liked someone from college?”
I typed haan. She asked, “Who?”
I was still typing… then she sent, “Chhod, I shouldn’t ask.”
I never sent the message.

Next day in college, everything felt same but also not. She saw me, smiled little, then went back to her friends. No awkwardness. No questions.
I kept thinking maybe she knows. Maybe she guessed. But she never asked again.
We still talk sometimes. Notes, exams, memes. Late replies now. Earlier it was instant. She watches my stories within minutes. Never replies.
Once I almost told her. My finger was on send. Then I remembered how easily she said “chhod” that night.
So I didn’t.
Maybe she was just curious. Maybe she felt something too and got scared. Or maybe I imagined everything because I wanted it to be true.
We sit in same canteen. Same college. Same life. But that message is still unsent.
And I don’t know if keeping quiet saved our friendship… or killed something before it even started.

I saw him first in my SY sitting in the IT library. I liked him quietly for six months - stolen glances in between classes, accidental eye contact that never lasted more than a second. When I finally confessed, he came to my Python lab even though he didn’t have to. Sat next to me. Smiled. Blushed. Moved closer. Our legs touched and neither of us pulled away.

He asked why I liked him for so long. Said he wasn’t a “good boy.” Mentioned things that should’ve scared me - but didn’t. When I teased him with his ring, he let me keep it. When I complimented him, he hid his smile like it meant too much.

I asked if I could hold his hand. He hesitated… then gave it to me anyway.

Then he leaned in and asked,
“So what exactly do you want from me?”
I never answered.
And that’s where everything changed.

I smiled and told him I didn’t know, because I was scared of hearing his answer first. He laughed like he understood more than he let on. Said if he didn’t care, he wouldn’t be sitting there, in my lab , holding my hand under a desk with cameras watching.

He asked to hang out sometime. I said not yet. He nodded, didn’t push.
Before leaving, he told me not to overthink then disappeared into his own world.
That night, he sent one message. A heart reaction. Nothing more.

Seven days later, he still watches me from a distance, my stories, my silences but never steps closer.

And I’m left wondering, was that the beginning of something… or the moment it quietly ended?

After diploma, I transferred to sy and quickly became acquainted with the neighbour in my dorm, a girl who was a design student. She lived in a 1rk. Despite the rumors circulating among the guys about her offering handjobs for 500 & bj for 3k, I initially ignored them. 

She seemed dedicated to her studies, often found in the library, and didn't socialize much, which didn't fit the stereotype of someone engaging in such activities. However, as the semester progressed, I started to notice that guys frequently visited her room, which piqued my curiosity.

It was the last week of the sem-4, and with  upcoming OS exam, I was feeling the stress. One of my stress reactions is becoming quite horny. The rumors about the her kept playing in my mind, so I decided to test their validity. I then made up a question about OS and knocked on her door. After she explained it, she asked if there was anything else I needed. When she said, "Anything at all?" I hesitated, but she seemed to understand my hesitation. "You brought money?" she asked, and I nodded. She tapped her desk, and I laid the money out so she could count it. I sat in her chair, and she removed her tshirt. The rumors were true. She used tissues to cover the head of my cock when I finished. What started as a one-time thing turned into a recurring scenario, with me visiting her about four times in last 3 months.

I want to share something that happened in my college a while back. I’m not mentioning the name of the institution, but I still can’t forget something that happened last year when I was a fresher. I won’t name the college, but it’s a very reputed institution where most students are JEE Advanced qualified so naturally, people expect discipline, maturity, and a higher standard of behavior.

Our college is known to be strict about ragging. Because of repeated past issues (mostly involving boys), the anti-ragging monitoring period was extended from 28 days to 4 months. Even then, things went wrong.

This time, it wasn’t the boys it was senior girls. Many freshers, especially girls, refused to address seniors as ma’am or sir (which is actually a rule here). The seniors felt disrespected. To make things worse, some senior boys were giving a lot of attention to just a few freshers, which created jealousy and ego clashes among the senior girls.

Once the official anti-ragging period ended, a group of senior girls called the fresher girls into the bathroom (since there were no CCTVs) and forced them into humiliating acts. They made them dance on the commode and bring food from the night canteen. one of my friends, who was close with the seniors, was actually the one sharing the freshers’ numbers.

The juniors were devastated. Many cried a lot, and the case eventually reached the higher authorities. Some seniors were about to be debarred for 28 days, but certain professors stepped in and prevented strict punishment. After this, the fresher girls’ group completely broke apart, and their mental health was badly affected.

It’s been a year, but this incident still stays in my mind. At first, I thought ragging was just a tradition or a bonding exercise, but witnessing this changed my perspective. Even in a place filled with JEE Advanced qualified students, ego and power games can create lasting scars.

I don’t know when it started, but ab college aana sirf lectures ke liye nahi hota. Tumhari ek glimpse mil jaaye din ban jaata hai. We’ve never really talked properly, but somehow you’re a part of my daily routine  Weird hai, but true.

I never thought Design Building ke bahar khade hoke kisi ka wait karna meri daily habit ban jaayega. Tum tuck shop se kuch lene jaate ho aur main bas pretend karta hoon ki casually phone scroll kar raha hoon. Truth is, tumhari ek glimpse mil jaaye toh pura din thoda better lagta hai.

don’t know when you became such a big part of my college life. Kabhi Raj Mess ke bahar line mein khade hote hue, kabhi SOC building ke stairs pe tumhe haste hue dekh leta hoon bas wahi moment poora din set kar deta hai. We don’t talk much, but somehow you’re always there in my head. College aaye the degree ke liye, par tum mil gaye… syllabus se zyada confusing ho gaya sab.
It’s funny how people talk about “vibes” and “positivity” but back-bitching starts the moment someone leaves the room. College ne ek cheez sikhayi smile doesn’t always mean respect.

I think you’re the president of some business club… I’m not even sure which one, but the way people listen when you speak makes it obvious. I really like you, even though we’ve never properly talked. Maybe you’ll never read this, but if you do 
just know someone admires you quietly from the crowd.
